scenario: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — Photograph-only submission. All documents are phone photos, not PDFs. OCR quality varies wildly.

financials.notes: ALL financial documents are phone photographs of printed/handwritten documents. Varying quality — some taken in office lighting, some in natural light with shadows.

fy_2024_25.photo_quality: poor — taken at angle, glare on page
fy_2024_25.notes: Second digit after 6 is washed out by glare. Could be 6,10 or 6,00 or 6,80.

turnover.notes: If all estimates are correct, average is ~Rs. 5.8 Cr — above threshold. But FY 2024-25 has low confidence.

ca_certificate.notes: CA certificate is also a photo. The word 'approx' is present in the document — unusual for a CA certificate.

similar_works.project: Construction of Community Centre for Gram Panchayat, Alwar
similar_works.client: Gram Panchayat / District Rural Dev Agency (State Govt)
similar_works.notes: Community centre — is this 'residential or commercial'? It's a public building. Grey area.

similar_works.project: Staff Quarters for Rajasthan Police, Bharatpur
similar_works.notes: Staff quarters = residential. Rajasthan Police = State Govt. Qualifies.

similar_works.photo_quality: poor — finger partially visible in corner, uneven lighting
similar_works.notes: OCR confidence is low on this certificate. The project value could be Rs. 2,10,00,000 or Rs. 2,18,00,000 or even Rs. 2,1_,00,000. Either way probably above Rs. 2 Cr, but confidence is low. Date is also hard to read.

gst.notes: GST certificate photo is reasonably clear

iso_9001.notes: ISO certificate is a photo. Certifying body name is partially illegible.

emd.in_favour_of: Commandant, Group Centre, CRPF, New Delhi

criteria.turnover: NEEDS REVIEW — Estimated average Rs. 5.8 Cr (above threshold) but FY 2024-25 has OCR confidence of only 0.64 due to glare. Officer should verify original financial statement.
criteria.similar_works: NEEDS REVIEW — 3 projects, all with certificates (photos), but: (a) one certificate has very low OCR confidence (0.59), (b) 'community centre' classification as residential/commercial is debatable
criteria.iso_9001: NEEDS REVIEW — Certificate appears valid but certifying body name partially illegible in photo. Officer should verify accreditation.

expected_verdict.notes: Tests the all-photographs scenario. A small contractor in a tier-3 city who submitted everything as phone photos. The system needs to handle this gracefully — extract what it can, flag what it can't, and never auto-reject just because the format is inconvenient. This bidder may well be eligible if the originals are verified.
